title: Bridge Essay: Perspectives on Rehabilitation Using Non-invasive Brain Stimulation Based on Sec

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.

**Perspectives on Rehabilitation Using Non-invasive Brain Stimulation Based on Second-Person Neuroscience of Teaching-Learning Interactions.**. Front Psychol.. PMID: 35069374. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2021.789637. Recent advances in second-person neuroscience have allowed the underlying neural mechanisms involved in teaching-learning interactions to be better understood. Teaching is not merely aone-way transfer of information from teacher to student; it is acomplex interaction that requires metacognitive and mentalizing skills to understand others' intentions and integrate information regarding oneself and others.
